description_long: This mosque is a square, single-story structure with a flat roof and a prominent central dome. The dome is ribbed and sits atop a raised drum, featuring a decorative finial at its peak. The building is constructed from a light brown stone material, with large arched openings on each visible side, allowing entry and light into the interior. The front facade is distinguished by a set of stairs leading up to a recessed entrance flanked by two large arches, and a balcony-like element above the main doorway. The roof is bordered by a low parapet, and the overall design is simple yet elegant, with minimal ornamentation. The structure evokes traditional Indo-Islamic architectural influences, particularly in the dome and arch shapes.
description: A square, stone mosque with a ribbed central dome, arched entrances, and a flat roof bordered by a parapet. The front features stairs and a balcony-like element above the main doorway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with stairs, two large arches, and a balcony-like structure above the doorway.
description_view_1: Angled front-left view revealing the left side archway and the full width of the stairs, with the dome centered above.
description_view_2: Rear view with the dome centered, showing the back arches and the flat roof, lacking the prominent entrance features.
description_view_3: Angled rear-right view highlighting the right side arches and the dome, with the back of the building visible.
attribute_dome: One large, ribbed dome centered on the roof, medium height, with a decorative finial at the top.
attribute_architectural_style: Indo-Islamic, with a ribbed dome, arched entrances, and a square plan.
